purpose.summary: Make the QSOE Rust migration measurable, reversible, and reviewable instead of a rewrite for its own sake.
whyRust: Reduce concrete C failure modes in parsers, state machines, resource servers, and image tooling.
Use Rust type, ownership, and Result-based error handling where they improve maintenance and review quality.
Keep high-risk boot, loader, spawn, capability, and kernel-adjacent code in C until boundaries are proven.
purpose.operatingRule: Every Rust candidate needs a selector, C rollback path, host tests, runtime or boot evidence, and documentation before it can become a default. C is not removed just because a Rust version exists.

statusSummary.currentFocus: Host qrvfs writer evidence before a Rust-default writer release candidate.
statusSummary.nextConcreteGate: Add triple-indirect stress coverage for mkfs-qrv-rs, then create a default-writer RC with explicit C rollback.
statusSummary.fullRewriteInventory: Incomplete. The current roadmap tracks active migration candidates and task-manager risk classes; a full OS-wide C inventory is still a future documentation task.

components.name: Host qrvfs tools
components.nextGate: Keep C mkfs-qrv as default until mkfs-qrv-rs has triple-indirect stress coverage and a default-writer RC with rollback.
components.notes: The read-only inspector is Rust-default RC. The writer is opt-in and has fixture, production-root, target-initialization, and live virtio /usr evidence.

components.name: slogger service
components.nextGate: Keep C rollback until the retirement checklist and a separate removal PR are satisfied.
components.notes: Rust-default RC path is accepted; no C retirement is approved.

components.name: devb-virtio block driver
components.nextGate: Keep C rollback until the retirement checklist and a separate removal PR are satisfied.
components.notes: Rust driver exposes /dev/vblk0 and supports the /usr file-read smoke.

components.name: pipe service
components.nextGate: Keep C rollback until the retirement checklist and a separate removal PR are satisfied.
components.notes: The Rust data-path smoke proves pipe(2) write/read and EOF behavior.

components.name: test_msgpass helper
components.nextGate: Keep C rollback until the retirement checklist and a separate removal PR are satisfied.
components.notes: The Rust helper passes the existing suite [msgpass] section with QSOE/L-specific skip handling.

components.name: tm_procfs task-manager pilot
components.nextGate: Keep C rollback until the retirement checklist and a separate removal PR are satisfied.
components.notes: This is the selected Phase 9 task-manager pilot because it is diagnostic and avoids direct seL4 invocation code.

roadmapPhases.name: Baseline and safety net
roadmapPhases.objective: Make the current C system reproducible and measurable before Rust enters the image.

roadmapPhases.name: Host tool hardening
roadmapPhases.objective: Use fixtures and Rust host tools to reduce image-building risk while keeping C tools as oracles.

roadmapPhases.name: Rust toolchain spike
roadmapPhases.objective: Prove Rust can build QSOE-compatible userland artifacts.

roadmapPhases.name: ABI and resource-server bindings
roadmapPhases.objective: Define reusable Rust/C boundaries for services.

roadmapPhases.name: First in-guest service
roadmapPhases.objective: Replace slogger behind a rollback selector.

roadmapPhases.name: Promote resource-server patterns
roadmapPhases.objective: Turn service lessons into reusable scaffolding.

roadmapPhases.name: Driver pilot
roadmapPhases.objective: Validate Rust against real virtio device state.

roadmapPhases.name: Shared parsers and format libraries
roadmapPhases.objective: Move parser-heavy logic into safe, reusable crates.

roadmapPhases.name: Additional services and drivers
roadmapPhases.objective: Migrate bounded services, drivers, utilities, and test helpers.

roadmapPhases.name: Task-manager pilots
roadmapPhases.objective: Migrate pure or diagnostic task-manager modules without touching spawn, capability, relocation, or loader paths.

roadmapPhases.name: High-risk runtime and kernel paths
roadmapPhases.objective: Reassess loader, capability, spawn, libc, and kernel work only after smaller Rust boundaries are proven.

candidateBacklog.name: Task-manager CPIO archive model
candidateBacklog.notes: Pure newc walking, symlink resolution, directory iteration, and existence checks over caller-owned bytes.

candidateBacklog.name: Task-manager shebang parser
candidateBacklog.notes: Pure parser used by tm_spawn when scripts are executed.

candidateBacklog.name: Task-manager ELF view parser
candidateBacklog.notes: Read-only parser, but consumed by relocation and loader flow.

candidateBacklog.name: LQ task-manager FDT parser
candidateBacklog.notes: Minimal big-endian device-tree scanner for /chosen, compatible strings, and properties.

candidateBacklog.name: LQ task-manager sysmap builder
candidateBacklog.notes: Builds the read-only PSYS page mapped into children.

candidateBacklog.name: Task-manager /sys model
candidateBacklog.notes: Read-only model for board, cmdline, osname, version, and builddate.

candidateBacklog.name: Task-manager path registry
candidateBacklog.notes: Every open and device registration depends on this namespace tree.

candidateBacklog.name: Task-manager credentials policy
candidateBacklog.notes: Pure cwd, umask, uid/gid mutation, and permission checks.

candidateBacklog.name: LQ resource DB accounting
candidateBacklog.notes: Fixed-pool resource-range allocation, split/merge, and rollback on partial attach.

candidateBacklog.name: Simple pseudo-devices
candidateBacklog.notes: Small read/write/stat handlers served through taskman IO.

candidateBacklog.name: Task-manager logging formatter
candidateBacklog.notes: Freestanding format subset and seL4 debug-console sink.

candidateBacklog.name: Spawn, capability, relocation, and loader paths
candidateBacklog.notes: Not early Rust candidates. Require dedicated boundary review and broader loader/capability evidence.

candidateBacklog.name: Kernel Rust
candidateBacklog.notes: Near-term Rust in kernel code is rejected by current decision; only fixture and audit candidates are documented.

candidateBacklog.name: Full OS-wide C inventory
candidateBacklog.notes: Needed to give an honest count of all remaining C components outside the active migration candidates.

policyGates.name: Selector
policyGates.description: Rust implementation must be selectable without removing the C implementation.

policyGates.name: C rollback
policyGates.description: A one-command C rollback path must exist and be tested.

policyGates.name: Host tests
policyGates.description: Pure logic, parser, and model code should have host tests before guest wiring.

policyGates.name: Runtime evidence
policyGates.description: Image-level or service-level smoke tests must prove the behavior in QSOE.

policyGates.name: Retirement review
policyGates.description: Removing C requires the retirement checklist and a separate removal PR.
